summarization.a: The figure illustrates a reaction pathway involving the dissociation of DSBAS (Dihydrogen Silicate Borate Ammonium Salt) into Si(OH)<sub>2</sub><sup>*</sup> through various transition states (TS) and intermediates.

data_extraction.a:
| Reaction Pathway | Energy (kcal/mol) |
|----------------------------|----------------|
| OH* (DSBAS Dissociation)   | 0              |
| TS (DSBAS Dissociation)    | 6.1            |
| SiH₃                                              | -5             |
| TS (H₂ elimination Path a) | 36.8           |
| TS (H₂ elimination Path b) | 41.9           |
| TS (H₂ elimination Path c) | 37.1           |
| TS (oxidation)             | 12.7           |
| SiH₂                                 | -25            |
| SiH₂(OH)*                   | -121           |
| TS (Oxidation)             |-109.2          |
| SiH(OH)₂                         | -225           |
| TS (Oxidation)             | -219.5         |
| Si(OH)₃                             | -340           |
| TS (H₂O elimination Path a)| -319.2         |
| TS (H₂O elimination Path b)| -310           |
| TS (H₂O elimination Path c)| -305.5         |
| Si(OH)₂* (Path a)          |  -330          |
| Si(OH)₂* (Path b)          |  -340          |
| Si(OH)₂* (Path c)          |  -350          |

a.question: Does the potential energy profile depict the whole ALD cycle?
Do other reactions take place during the dissociation of DSBAS?
a.answer: The ALD process is initiated by the dissociative chemisorption of DSBAS, which is both thermochemically and kinetically facile. Elimination of H2 through reaction between the resultant −SiH3 species and a nearby surface hydroxyl group is kinetically prohibitive. Thus, further reactions along this pathway can be essentially ruled out.

a.question: Why only crystalline SiO2 is formed?
a.answer:
Although there are three possible pathways for the −Si(OH)3 species to react with the adjacent surface hydroxyl groups, only one of them is both
thermodynamically and kinetically favorable, which leads to growing a SiO2 thin film in a crystalline structure

a.question: Are the films suited for applications?
a.answer: The thin film deposited with DSBAS and ozone to be dense and conformal and suitable for  low-temperature deposition of SiO2 films.
